Secondary Endpoint(s): {"Secondary Endpoint(s)": "PSA progression-free survival (defined as time from randomisation to the earliest PSA progression according to the Prostate Cancer Working Group 2 criteria [PCWG2], clinical progression, or death from any cause, whichever occurred first, or the last known date of follow-up without PSA progression), clinical progression-free survival (defined as the earliest sign of radiographic progression using PCWG2 for bone lesions and RECIST [version 1.1] for soft tissue lesions, symptoms attributable to cancer progression, or initiation of another anti-cancer treatment for prostate cancer), health-related quality of life (HRQOL), health outcomes relative to costs, and safety."}

Type of Therapy: {"Type of Therapy": "Participants were randomly assigned to receive either testosterone suppression plus oral enzalutamide (160 mg once per day) or testosterone suppression plus a weaker standard oral non-steroidal antiandrogen (bicalutamide, nilutamide, or flutamide). Concurrent intravenous docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously) was allowed for up to six cycles in both groups at the discretion of participants and physicians."}
COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ: {"COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ": "The limitations of ENZAMET, including small sample sizes for subgroup analyses, open-label design, absence of randomisation to concurrent docetaxel, and post-hoc nature of some exploratory analyses, prevent definitive conclusions."}
